name: Explain Yourself in Code
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 4: Comments
problem: Developers write comments to explain what confusing code does instead of rewriting the code to be self-explanatory, leading to comments that drift out of sync with the code they describe.
steps: Before writing a comment, ask whether the code can be rewritten to make the comment unnecessary.
Extract a condition into a boolean-returning function with a descriptive name.
Extract a block of code into a function whose name states its purpose.
Replace magic numbers and literals with named constants.
Reserve comments for information that truly cannot be expressed in code (legal notices, algorithm citations, intent rationale).
